Updated: 16:04 EDT

Lets hope Reform wins the by-election. Kier has been the worst PM ever. However, Andy Burnham will be even worse.

The Greater Manchester mayor has previously heavily hinted but ducked out of confirming he would stand against Sir Keir Starmer if he wins a Westminster seat on June 18. This afternoon he told the Guardian he was '100 per cent' focused on the June 18 battle, seen as a straight fight between him and Reform's Robert Kenyon, and branded speculation about how he would run the country as 'weird'. But he also went on to outline how he would bring more left-wingers into his cabinet and prioritise sorting out the creaking social care system. Then tonight, in a by-election special edition of the BBC's Question Time in the Greater Manchester seat he confirmed that he would go for the top job if he won.

How I look this good at 64: My weight soared after the menopause and I was left with a flabby middle and weak body. This is the no-jab routine that transformed my health - anyone can do it

Like many women entering their 60s, Hilary Clegg had spent years trying different forms of exercise, from running to fitness classes, always striving to stay healthy. Riot breaks out in Brussels as student protesters rampage through streets and smash up homes amid violent clashes with police

Thousands of protesters clashed with police and rampaged through the streets of Brussels on Wednesday as demonstrations over education cuts descended into violent disorder in the heart of the Belgian capital. Footage from the scene showed masked demonstrators hurling fireworks, setting fires in the road and smashing property as riot police deployed water cannons in an attempt to regain control of the city centre. At least 1,000 people gathered in Brussels to protest against planned austerity measures affecting French-language education, but the atmosphere quickly turned hostile as groups of protesters confronted officers and caused widespread damage.

Isabelle Sapherson-Moralee, 22, known as Izzy, died after her body shut down after taking the class B drug for five years. Her devastated mother Ann Moralee told how she battled in vain for 18 months to get help for her daughter and tried warning health officials that she would die from it. She said her daughter was treated poorly by some staff and treated only as an addict, despite the fact she 'wanted' to get better and, in her view, lacked capacity.

Daniel Frost, 44, appeared at Southampton Magistrates' Court today where he pleaded guilty to violent disorder and possessing a dog lead with a metal carabiner. A second man, 50-year-old Matt Styler, has pleaded not guilty to assaulting a police officer. It’s nice to see William showing his support for one of Britain’s great institutions. The importance of pubs cannot be overstated. For many communities, they provide a vital social hub — a place where people can meet friends, make new ones, share stories, and combat loneliness. At their best, pubs help foster a sense of belonging and community that is increasingly hard to find elsewhere.

Prince William, 43, carried out an engagement to highlight how British pubs are crucial to the country's culture at the Prince of Peckham, where he gamely pulled a pint and learnt about the landlord Clement Ogbonnaya's initiative to combat loneliness. As he was leaving the pub in his chauffeur-driven car, a well-wisher shouted 'I love you bro' and ran up to the future King for a handshake.

Maggie Day, 25, was filming the intense storm in the village of Glanaman in Carmarthenshire, Wales, on May 27 when she unexpectedly captured the powerful strike hitting the roof of the semi-detached house across the road.   • My father in-law had Alzheimer's and my mother in-law looked after him at home without any outside help apart from my wife and close family. When the day came for him to go into a care home. It was one of the saddest days watching my mother in-law grieving him going. But it was the best thing that ever happened in regards to him being cared for by care home staff. I really wonder how my mother in-law managed for so long. We could see her going down hill but her love for him put that aside. So please don't be too quick to have a swipe at someone going through the same situation. It's heartbreaking to see .

  • I feel sorry for her ex-husband and kids. It’s great that she’s working on herself but alcoholism is an extremely selfish disease and the family often suffers. When they’re drinking it’s all about them and when they are trying to be sober it’s still all about them. You’re whole existence and daily experiences are based their sobriety (or not). There’s a fine line between being supportive and enabling, and getting blamed for not being supportive enough. Then when they do finally get sober for extended amount of time they get empowered and decide they don’t need you anymore. You fought and fought all for nought. Wish her the best, hope she can rebuild a solid relationship with her kids but the past painful experiences they will always remember.
  • As beautiful as Alan's Georgian home & gardens are, I would have had a For Sale sign up ASAP had I lived there. Part of the beauty would have been the open view of the fields, and also the sense of space. Imagine how noisy the area will become once the new properties are built & sold. They likelihood is that every new house will have a car, possibly two or even three, revving up every morning, lawn mowers making a racket at weekends, children shreking as they play outside - it's going to be an invasion of noise. It won't bother the folks who are actually living on the new development, but it isn't what the existing community signed up for when they bought their homes, many decades ago. Noise pollution. There ought to be a law against it !

  • King Charles and Princess Catherine are incredibly brave to come out that they had cancer especially Princess Catherine who was harassed by the media and bullied online until she had no choice but to tell the world about it. I wish everyone who suffers with cancer the very best as it the most worst thing in the world to have.
  • Thank god. Hopefully now she'll shut up about Liam.
  • I'm battling concussion and a nasty eye infection after being thrown off a golf cart in terrifying accident - better photograph myself and post it online, quick! Parasites.
  • Amber looks awful!! So thin!
  • A career based on one song, several covers, several affairs and two well-known husbands.
  • Justice for Henry Nowak. All that matters now!!
  • Sawalha is a thick left winger with no common sense and no morals.
  • “Dead at 60” is such a crass headline. Could you not have said passed away? She’s someone’s loved one.
  • Wonderful tribute to a wonderful avtress. RIP Catherine.
  • I've never really understood her talent,other than being very pretty once,her acting is ok,her music career was hardly singer songwriter stuff,amazing what being attractive can do.

total idiot

A tourist suffered serious injuries after he was violently mauled by a wild bear in Romania's Carpathian mountains.  Georgi Bizhev, a Bulgarian holidaymaker, was driving along a mountain road near the Vidraru Dam in central Romania last Wednesday when he decided to stop after spotting a mother bear and her cub.  The 46-year-old tossed food scraps at the animals and began taking pictures and videos of them, when the mother bear suddenly launched a ferocious attack on him.
  • 301 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Moment screaming tourist is mauled by a bear which smashes car window

The five-bedroom detached house is one of only three properties on Cross Island in Strangford Lough, Northern Ireland.

Nice news story of the day. I wish you both all the happiness in the world

Jimmy Cooper, 92, and Andrea Cullen, 85, said 'I Do' at Charlton Grange in Shepperton, Surrey, on April 11 and enjoyed a delightful ceremony. The loved-up pair met in 2016 while living near each other in supported accommodation, and soon formed a close bond. While Andrea moved into the care home in February, her now-husband has been visiting her daily.
  • 72 comments
  • share Couple, aged 92 and 85, marry in care home after a decade together
